info.title: Webhooks
info.description:
Manages webhook subscriptions. Subscribe to entity change events and receive
real-time notifications at your endpoint.

post.description:
Creates a new webhook subscription to be actively notified on each change in the specified entities.

As part of subscription creation, Productboard validates the notification URL:
it must use `https`, have a publicly resolvable host, and not point to localhost,
loopback, site-local, or internal addresses.

**Scope requirements**: the OAuth2 application must have the scopes required by all requested event types.

Sent whenever a subscribed entity change event occurs.

- Timeout: **5 seconds** (end-to-end)
- Return any `2XX` status to acknowledge receipt
- Prefer `204 No Content` — response body is ignored
- Failed deliveries are retried with exponential backoff (up to 8 attempts: 1m, 3m, 9m, ...)
- Process notifications asynchronously and return immediately to avoid timeouts
- Too many timeouts or failures may result in Productboard **disabling the subscription** with notification loss

**Security**: configure `data.fields.notification.headers.authorization` to authenticate
incoming requests from Productboard.

200.description: Any `2XX` status is treated as a successful delivery. `204 No Content` is preferred.

get.description:
Returns all webhook subscriptions for the workspace. Returns up to 100 items per page by default.

Paginated using cursor-based pagination. Follow `links.next` in the response to fetch the next page.
When `links.next` is `null`, you have reached the last page.

**OAuth2 isolation**: OAuth2 access tokens only see subscriptions created by the same OAuth2 application.
Public API access tokens see all workspace subscriptions.

200.description: A paginated list of webhook subscriptions

get.description:
Returns a single webhook subscription by ID.

**OAuth2 isolation**: returns `404` if the subscription was created by a different OAuth2 application
or by a public API access token (existence is masked for security).

delete.description:
Permanently deletes a webhook subscription. No further notifications will be sent after deletion. **OAuth2 isolation**: returns `404` if the subscription was created by a different OAuth2 application
or by a public API access token.

PageCursor.name: pageCursor
PageCursor.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the value from `links.next` to fetch the next page.

WebhookType.description: Resource type identifier for webhook subscriptions.

WebhookReference.description: Lightweight reference returned after creating a webhook subscription.

id.description: Unique identifier of the created webhook subscription.

self.description: URL to fetch the full webhook subscription.

WebhookReferenceResponse.description: Response envelope for a newly created webhook subscription.

id.description: Unique identifier for this webhook subscription.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the subscription was created.

WebhookResponse.description: Response envelope for a single webhook subscription.

WebhookListResponse.description: Response envelope for a paginated list of webhook subscriptions.

WebhookSubscriptionFields.description: Domain attributes for creating a webhook subscription.

events.description:
Event types to subscribe to. At least one required; no duplicates allowed.
The full list of supported values is defined in the `WebhookEventType` schema.

WebhookSubscriptionResponseFields.description:
Domain attributes returned in webhook subscription responses.
Note: `notification.headers.authorization` is intentionally absent from responses to protect secrets.

events.description: Event types that trigger this subscription.

WebhookCreateRequestBody.description: Request body for creating a webhook subscription.

WebhookSubscriptionLinks.description: Links for this webhook subscription resource.

self.description: URL to retrieve this webhook subscription.

WebhookEventType.description: Identifies the type of entity change that triggers a webhook notification.

WebhookNotificationConfig.description: Where and how Productboard sends notifications (used in create requests).

url.description:
HTTPS endpoint to receive notifications. Requirements:
- Must use `https` (encrypted)
- Must be publicly reachable (no `localhost`, private IPs, or internal Kubernetes addresses)

WebhookNotificationConfigResponse.description: Notification configuration as returned in responses (secret omitted).

WebhookPayloadVersion.description: Version of the notification payload structure. Currently only `1` is supported.

WebhookNotificationHeaders.description:
Custom headers included in every outgoing request from Productboard to your endpoint
(notifications). Use this to authenticate incoming requests.

authorization.description:
Raw value sent in the `Authorization` header. Supports any scheme:
- `Basic aGVsbG86d29ybGQ=`
- `Bearer <token>`
- Any custom value

WebhookNotificationPayloadV1.description: Notification payload delivered to the subscriber endpoint.

id.description:
Entity ID that changed. Absent for `hierarchy-entity.custom-field-value.updated`
and `feature-release-assignment.updated`.

updatedAttributes.description:
Names of the API fields that changed. Only present on `*.updated` events.
Uses public API field names (e.g., `status`, `name`, `parent`).

WebhookNotificationRequestBody.description: Request body delivered to the subscriber endpoint on each entity change event.

LinksTarget.description: Standard V1 link — points to the changed entity.

target.description: API URL of the entity whose change triggered this notification.

LinksFeatureNote.description: V1 link shape for insight events — references both feature and note.

ErrorSource.description: An object containing references to the source of the error. It helps with location of the problematic field or parameter.

pointer.description: JSON Pointer [RFC6901] to the associated entity in the request document.

parameter.description:
Query parameter causing the error.
Used when the error is related to a query parameter rather than request body.

Error.description: Individual error object containing the details and context.

code.description:
A unique, machine-readable and stable code that identifies this error.
Must be consistent across all services and provide clear categorization.
Format: `category.subcategory.specificError`

title.description:
A short, human-readable summary of the problem that doesn't change
from occurrence to occurrence of the problem. Should provide immediate
understanding of the error type.

detail.description:
A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
May include suggestions for resolution but should not expose sensitive information.

id.description:
A unique identifier for this specific occurrence of the problem.
It carries unique request/response ID which allows client
and server to reference the exact instance of the error
in logs or support communication.

errors.description: Array of error objects, multiple errors can be returned.

BearerAuth.description: API token authentication. Use your API token as the value of the Authorization header

OAuth2.description: OAuth 2.0 authentication. Use this for user-authorized access to Productboard API.

scopes.entities:read: Read access to entities (features, components, initiatives, etc.)
scopes.write:entities: Write access to create and modify entities

BadRequestResponse.description: Bad Request - Invalid input format or malformed request

errors.title: Request schema invalid
errors.detail: The request does not satisfy the required schema. Field 'type' is required but was not provided.

errors.title: Request schema invalid
errors.detail: Field 'fields/name' must be a string with maximum length of 255 characters, but received a number.

errors.title: Request malformed
errors.detail: The request body contains invalid JSON syntax. Expected ',' or '}' at line 5, column 12.

errors.title: Request malformed
errors.detail: The Content-Type header must be 'application/json'. Received 'text/plain'.

UnauthorizedResponse.description: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid authentication credentials

errors.title: Invalid authentication
errors.detail: The provided authentication credentials are invalid or have expired. Please obtain new credentials and try again.

errors.title: Invalid authentication
errors.detail: The authentication credentials have been revoked. Please contact your workspace administrator.

errors.title: Access denied
errors.detail: You do not have permission to perform this operation on the specified resource. Contact your workspace administrator to request access.

errors.title: Access denied
errors.detail: Your authentication token lacks the required OAuth scopes to perform this operation. Please re-authenticate with the necessary scopes.

errors.title: Access denied
errors.detail: Your user role does not permit this operation. Please contact your workspace administrator.

RequestTimeoutResponse.description: Request Timeout - The server did not receive a complete request within the allowed time

errors.title: Request timeout
errors.detail: The request took too long to process and was terminated. Try simplifying your request or try again later.

TooManyRequestsResponse.description: Too Many Requests - API rate limit exceeded, reduce request frequency and retry after the indicated time

errors.title: Rate limit exceeded
errors.detail: You have exceeded the allowed number of API requests. Please wait before making additional requests.

InternalServerErrorResponse.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected error occurred on the server, please retry or contact support

errors.title: Internal server error
errors.detail: An unexpected error occurred while processing your request. Please try again later. If the problem persists, contact support with the correlation ID.

UnprocessableEntityResponse.description: Unprocessable Entity - Validation failed (e.g., missing required fields, unknown fields)

errors.title: Validation failed
errors.detail: One or more fields failed validation.

errors.title: Validation failed
errors.detail: The field 'type' has an unsupported value.

errors.title: Referenced resource not found
errors.detail: The referenced resource could not be found or you do not have access to it.

NotFoundResponse.description: Not Found - The requested resource does not exist or is not accessible

errors.title: Resource not found
errors.detail: The requested resource could not be found or you do not have permission to access it.

errors.title: Route not found
errors.detail: The requested URL path does not match any defined API route. Please check the API documentation for valid endpoints.
